00029 
00030 #include <unistd.h>
00031 #include <string.h>
00032 #include <err.h>
00033 
00034 /*
00035  * cat - concatenate and print
00036  * Usage: cat [files]
00037  */
00038 
00039 
00040 
00041 /* Print a file that's already been opened. */
00042 static
00043 void
00044 docat(const char *name, int fd)
00045 {
00046         char buf[1024];
00047         int len, wr, wrtot;
00048 
00049         /*
00050          * As long as we get more than zero bytes, we haven't hit EOF.
00051          * Zero means EOF. Less than zero means an error occurred.
00052          * We may read less than we asked for, though, in various cases
00053          * for various reasons.
00054          */
00055         while ((len = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf)))>0) {
00056                 /*
00057                  * Likewise, we may actually write less than we attempted
00058                  * to. So loop until we're done.
00059                  */
00060                 wrtot = 0;
00061                 while (wrtot < len) {
00062                         wr = write(STDOUT_FILENO, buf+wrtot, len-wrtot);
00063                         if (wr<0) {
00064                                 err(1, "stdout");
00065                         }
00066                         wrtot += wr;
00067                 }
00068         }
00069         /*
00070          * If we got a read error, print it and exit.
00071          */
00072         if (len<0) {
00073                 err(1, "%s", name);
00074         }
00075 }
00076 
00077 /* Print a file by name. */
00078 static
00079 void
00080 cat(const char *file)
00081 {
00082         int fd;
00083 
00084         /*
00085          * "-" means print stdin.
00086          */
00087         if (!strcmp(file, "-")) {
00088                 docat("stdin", STDIN_FILENO);
00089                 return;
00090         }
00091 
00092         /*
00093          * Open the file, print it, and close it.
00094          * Bail out if we can't open it.
00095          */
00096         fd = open(file, O_RDONLY);
00097         if (fd<0) {
00098                 err(1, "%s", file);
00099         }
00100         docat(file, fd);
00101         close(fd);
00102 }
00103 
00104 
00105 int
00106 main(int argc, char *argv[])
00107 {
00108         if (argc==1) {
00109                 /* No args - just do stdin */
00110                 docat("stdin", STDIN_FILENO);
00111         }
00112         else {
00113                 /* Print all the files specified on the command line. */
00114                 int i;
00115                 for (i=1; i<argc; i++) {
00116                         cat(argv[i]);
00117                 }
00118         }
00119         return 0;
00120 }
